candidates.title: RIDE-Bench: Compute-Normalized Diagnostic Benchmark for Revision in Diffusion LMs
candidates.summary: Use honest compute accounting and failure-mode analysis to explain when revision helps, when it hurts, and why stronger observational signals are not automatically better controllers.
hypotheses: H1: compute-normalized ranking differs from nominal-step ranking
H2: revision benefit is task-dependent and tracks recoverability
H3: entropy / instability / calibration are stronger observers than controllers
H4: syntax guards fix shallow errors more than deep execution errors
candidates.pilot_focus: Phase-diagram slice on GSM8K + HumanEval, benefit buckets, observer-vs-controller diagnostics
candidates.why_now: This candidate directly absorbs the strongest evidence already collected instead of spending more budget defending a method that failed its main gate.

candidates.title: Simple-Enough Beats Clever: Minimal Intervention Baselines for DLM Sampling
candidates.summary: Test whether one or two minimal inference-time interventions can match or beat more complex revision policies under matched compute.
hypotheses: A very small policy family can be Pareto-competitive with complex revision methods
Much of the apparent gain from complex methods disappears after honest compute accounting
candidates.pilot_focus: Implement one minimal intervention baseline and compare against DNB / Prophet / Entropy on GSM8K-100

candidates.title: Factorization Error Audit and Dependency-Aware Grouping
candidates.summary: Treat local revision failure as a symptom of deeper factorization error and study whether dependency-aware grouping is a better future method direction.
hypotheses: Revision failure concentrates on samples with stronger dependency mismatch
Offline dependency proxies can predict where token-independent updates break down
candidates.pilot_focus: Offline audit linking dependency proxies to revision failure buckets before any new sampler implementation

candidates.title: TIGER: Task-Gated Instability-Guided Editing for Diffusion Language Models
candidates.summary: Dropped as the front-stage method claim after tying entropy revision on GSM8K and failing to beat CORE-proxy under honest compute accounting.
hypotheses: Task gating rescues code without hurting reasoning
candidates.pilot_focus: No further expansion unless later diagnostics uncover a sharply narrower win condition

pivot_triggers.tiger_to_diag: TIGER tied entropy revision and remained well below CORE-proxy under matched compute
pivot_triggers.diag_to_minimal: If the diagnostic phase fails to show a clear response surface but minimal policies remain competitive
pivot_triggers.diag_to_factorization: If benefit buckets cluster around dependency-sensitive failures that observational signals cannot explain
